heading: Definitions
label: 620e Definitions

children.text: The term “acquire” means to come into possession of, whether directly or indirectly, through a sale, trade, exchange, or other transaction, and the term “acquisition” means the act of acquiring.

children.label: A
children.text: held in trust by the United States for the benefit of any Indian tribe or individual,

children.label: B
children.text: held by any Indian tribe or individual subject to a restriction by the United States against alienation, or

children.label: C
children.text: held by any Native Corporation as defined in .section 1602 of title 43

children.heading: .—Minor violation
children.label: 3 .—Minor violation
children.text: The term “minor violation” means a violation, other than an intentional violation, involving a single contract, purchase order, processing facility, or log yard involving a quantity of logs that is less than 25 logs and has a total value (at the time of the violation) of less than $10,000.

children.heading: .—Northwestern private timber open market area
children.label: 4 .—Northwestern private timber open market area
children.text: The term “northwestern private timber open market area” means the State of Washington.

The term “person” means any individual, partnership, corporation, association, or other legal entity and includes any subsidiary, subcontractor, or parent company, and business affiliates where 1 affiliate controls or has the power to control the other or when both are controlled directly or indirectly by a third person.

children.label: A
children.text: held in trust by the United States for the benefit of any Indian tribe or individual,

children.label: B
children.text: held by any Indian tribe or individual subject to a restriction by the United States against alienation, or

children.label: C
children.text: held by any Native Corporation as defined in .section 1602 of title 43

children.label: A
children.text: held by the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: held in trust by the United States for the benefit of any Indian tribe or individual,

children.label: C
children.text: held by any Indian tribe or individual subject to a restriction by the United States against alienation, or

children.label: D
children.text: held by any Native Corporation as defined in .section 1602 of title 43

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture, with respect to Federal lands administered by that Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior with respect to Federal lands administered by that Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “unprocessed timber” means trees or portions of trees or other roundwood not processed to standards and specifications suitable for end product use.

children.label: i
children.text: Lumber or construction timbers, except Western Red Cedar, meeting current American Lumber Standards Grades or Pacific Lumber Inspection Bureau Export R or N list grades, sawn on 4 sides, not intended for remanufacture.

children.label: ii
children.text: Lumber, construction timbers, or cants for remanufacture, except Western Red Cedar, meeting current American Lumber Standards Grades or Pacific Lumber Inspection Bureau Export R or N list clear grades, sawn on 4 sides, not to exceed 12 inches in thickness.

children.label: iii
children.text: Lumber, construction timbers, or cants for remanufacture, except Western Red Cedar, that do not meet the grades referred to in clause (ii) and are sawn on 4 sides, with wane less than ¼ of any face, not exceeding 8¾ inches in thickness.

children.label: iv
children.text: Chips, pulp, or pulp products.

children.label: v
children.text: Veneer or plywood.

children.label: vi
children.text: Poles, posts, or piling cut or treated with preservatives for use as such.

children.label: vii
children.text: Shakes or shingles.

children.label: viii
children.text: Aspen or other pulpwood bolts, not exceeding 100 inches in length, exported for processing into pulp.

children.label: ix
children.text: Pulp logs, cull logs, and incidental volumes of grade 3 and 4 sawlogs processed at domestic pulp mills, domestic chip plants, or other domestic operations for the primary purpose of conversion of the logs into chips, or to the extent that a small quantity of such logs are processed, into other products at domestic processing facilities.

The acquisition of unprocessed timber from Federal lands west of the 100th meridian in the contiguous 48 States to be used in “substitution” for exported unprocessed timber originating from private lands means acquiring unprocessed timber from such Federal lands and engaging in exporting, or selling for export, unprocessed timber originating from private lands within the same geographic and economic area.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a violation by the original purchaser of unprocessed timber, an act or omission with respect to a single timber sale; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a violation of a subsequent purchaser of the timber, an act or omission with respect to an operation at a particular processing facility or log yard.
